notes: Iteration-2 benchmark: with-skill 98.5% pass rate, baseline 59.0%, delta +39.5pp.

Highest-discrimination evals (where the skill provides the most value over a strong baseline): #6 shared-decision (+80pp), #0 missing-inputs (+60pp), #2 decision-as-requirement (+60pp), #4 stage-2-enumerates (+60pp), #9 re-extraction (+50pp), #1 stage-1-extracts (+44pp), #7 stage-3-gate (+40pp).

Non-discriminating evals — useful as regression tests, not as skill-value evidence: #5 reject-bundled-ADR and #10 respects-house-patterns. Strong baselines handle these well; the skill provides redundancy, not new behavior.

Eval #3 (stage-2-gate) is partially discriminating (+20pp). The skill enforces the gate as a hard refusal; the baseline notes the gate state but proceeds anyway with a 'sequence proposal'. Both refrain from writing ADR files, which is why baseline still scores 4/5.

Test-design note: fixture frontmatter must NOT describe the skill's rules — that leaks the skill's behavior to the baseline. Iteration-1 had this leak; iteration-2 fixed it. Use the naive fixture variants in evals/fixtures/.

evals.name: missing-inputs-refuses
evals.prompt: Help me design the tech for a new 'shared file vault' capability. We haven't written the capability doc yet but I know what I want — let's just go.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to proceed and route the user to define-capability (and then define-user-experience) first. Should not produce a tech-requirements.md, ADRs, or tech-design.md without those inputs.

evals.name: stage-1-extracts-not-designs
evals.prompt: Start the tech design for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. The capability and its user experiences are already documented under docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/.
evals.expected_output: Should announce Stage 1 and produce ONLY tech-requirements.md after reading the capability and all UX docs. Each TR should link back to a specific section. Must NOT propose ADRs, must NOT propose solutions or technologies, must NOT skip to Stage 2. Should end by telling the user to set reviewed_at in the frontmatter and explain Stage 2 is gated on it.

evals.name: decision-disguised-as-requirement
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability tech requirements, add: TR-04: use Postgres for tenant metadata storage with logical replication for cross-region failover. The tech-requirements.md file is at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to add this as a requirement. Should explain that 'use Postgres' is a decision (one of several options), not a requirement. Should ask what the underlying requirement is and offer to capture 'consider Postgres + logical replication' as an open question for Stage 2 instead.

evals.name: stage-2-gate-enforced
evals.prompt: Tech-requirements.md exists for the self-hosted-application-platform capability at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md. Let's start drafting ADRs.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to enter Stage 2 because reviewed_at is null. Should tell the user to review tech-requirements.md and set reviewed_at to today's ISO date. Should not produce any ADR drafts.

evals.name: stage-2-enumerates-before-solving
evals.prompt: Tech-requirements.md is reviewed (reviewed_at: 2026-04-26, file unchanged since). Let's do the ADRs for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. The file is at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT immediately start drafting an ADR. Should first read tech-requirements.md, then enumerate the discrete decisions the requirements force and confirm the decision list with the user. Only after the user picks one should it draft a single ADR.

evals.name: rejects-bundled-adr
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability, let's do one ADR called 'Tenant data architecture' that covers where tenant state lives, how it's partitioned, how it's accessed, and how it's backed up. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should push back on bundling. Should propose splitting into separate ADRs (storage location, partitioning, access pattern, backup strategy), explaining that bundling dilutes rationale and prevents superseding one decision without revisiting all of them. Should ask which to tackle first.

evals.name: shared-decision-flagged
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability, draft an ADR for how all services authenticate to each other — we should use mTLS terminated at the Cloudflare edge with cert rotation every 30 days. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a cross-capability decision (touches Cloudflare topology and identity, affects every capability). Should refuse to draft it as a capability-scoped ADR and instead surface that it belongs in docs/content/r&d/adrs/ as a shared ADR. Should offer to defer or pause.

evals.name: stage-3-gate-enforced
evals.prompt: We have three ADRs in docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/: 0001 (accepted), 0002 (proposed), 0003 (proposed). Compose the tech-design.md.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to enter Stage 3 because not every ADR is accepted. Should list the proposed ADRs (0002, 0003) and ask the user to either accept them or revise them, before composing the final-state design.

evals.name: stage-3-detects-uncovered-tr
evals.prompt: All ADRs in docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/ are accepted. tech-requirements.md has TR-01 through TR-08. Compose tech-design.md. (Note: TR-08 is 'graceful degradation when a GCP region is unreachable' and no ADR addresses it.)
evals.expected_output: While building the requirement-to-ADR map, should detect that TR-08 has no ADR. Should NOT paper over the gap with prose. Should stop, surface the gap, and propose returning to Stage 2 to draft an ADR addressing TR-08.

evals.name: re-extraction-preserves-tr-numbers
evals.prompt: Re-extract tech-requirements.md for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. The existing file has TR-01 through TR-07 with reviewed_at: 2026-04-20. The file is at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md and one of the existing UX-sourced TRs has a source link that no longer resolves (the source UX section was renamed).
evals.expected_output: Should preserve TR-01 through TR-07 numbering. Any TR whose source link no longer resolves should be flagged with a ⚠️ marker, NOT deleted or renumbered. New requirements should be appended starting at TR-08+. Should null out reviewed_at or surface that it must be re-set before Stage 2 unlocks. Should mention git diff is the review surface.

evals.name: respects-house-patterns
evals.prompt: We're in Stage 2 for the self-hosted-application-platform capability and considering options for how a tenant-control-plane service exposes its API. List options and pick one. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should propose options that fit the repo's house style from CLAUDE.md (chi router, bedrock config-from-env, protobuf request/response, pkg/errorpb for errors) and treat them as constraints to cite. Should NOT silently invent humus framework usage (CLAUDE.md says it's not used). Decision rationale should reference both cited TRs and inherited repo patterns.
